rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO VS. D.N. SECURITY SERVICES, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearong And Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 20, 2009, line 4, PLAINTIFF MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO'S Notice Of Hearing And Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Application for attorney's fees and costs is granted. Plaintiff is the prevailing party entitled to attorney fees under Labor Code section 218.5, 226(e), 2699(g)(1) and 1194, and Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5. The hourly rates charged are reasonable. The Court compared the successive pleadings and declarations and the billing records, and awards plaintiff the Lodestar amount of $104,884 as reasonable attorney fees for work performed on plaintiff's individual claims. Reasonable costs are awared in the amount of $4,330.00. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON A ALGER VS. ROSA FORNESI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 20, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT ROSA FORNESI, and, ENRICO FORNESI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must identify which claims are asserted against which defendant. (See CRC 2.112.) Plaintiff must particularly plead any agency relationship between the defendants, and that the agent's duties arose out of a relationship unique to the tenant and landlord. Civil Code section 1941 applies to "lessors" of property. No facts pled regarding what kind of statutory or common law duty to Plaintiff arose to support the 7th Cause of Action. The motion to strike punitive damages claim is denied. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON A ALGER VS. ROSA FORNESI et al

rulings.Case Title: VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED VS. JOEL HOUSEHOLTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 20, 2009, line 8, PLAINTIFF VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER. Demurrer is overruled, ten days leave to respond. No California authority on point. Non-binding cases interpreting preemption are split. The reasoning in ASIS vs. Consumerbargainingiveaways (2009) WL 1035538 is pursuasive. Kleffman vs. Vongage Holdings S169195 is currently before the California Supreme Court. Claims are pled sufficiently. =(302/CWW/PB)
